aliases: Imperial Klans of America, Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
nodes.context: White supremacist, white nationalist, neo-Nazi paramilitary organization in the United States.

nodes.standard_name: Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
nodes.original_text_name: Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
nodes.context: A Klan group from which the IKA splintered.
Short-lived splinter group from the Knights of the KKK.
White power skinhead group that assisted the IKA with hate rock gatherings.
The broader Klan movement; the IKA describes itself as the sixth era of the Klan.

edges.target: Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1990s, Ron Edwards was the head of a Kentucky-based klavern (local unit) of the Knights of the Ku Klux Klan . In July 1994, he joined the short-lived Federation of Klans after it broke away from the Knights of the KKK
edges.context: Edwards was a member of the Knights of the Ku Klux Klan before founding the IKA.

edges.evidence_quote: In July 1994, he joined the short-lived Federation of Klans after it broke away from the Knights of the KKK
edges.context: Edwards joined the Federation of Klans before founding the IKA.

edges.evidence_quote: the Supreme White Alliance, a white power skinhead group that had been assisting the IKA with annual hate rock gatherings, decided to break ties with the IKA after Edwards' arrest.
edges.context: The Supreme White Alliance assisted the IKA with hate rock gatherings until breaking ties after Edwards' arrest.

edges.evidence_quote: The group describes itself as the sixth era of the Ku Klux Klan
edges.context: The IKA claims to be a continuation of the Ku Klux Klan.
